differences.title: 1. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Built for async, offers higher throughput for I/O bound tasks.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily synchronous, can be extended for async with specific configurations.

differences.title: 2. EASE OF USE
differences.leftDesc: Less code to write for basic APIs, easier to get started quickly.
differences.rightDesc: Includes many built-in features, requiring more initial configuration.

differences.title: 3. BUILT-IN FEATURES
differences.leftDesc: Specialized for building APIs, auto-generates docs.
differences.rightDesc: Includes ORM, admin panel, authentication, templating out-of-the-box.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easy to grasp for developers familiar with Python type hints and async.
differences.rightDesc: More concepts and components to learn for full-stack development.

differences.title: 5. COMMUNITY SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Newer framework with an active, but smaller, community.
differences.rightDesc: Established framework with a vast, supportive community and extensive resources.

differences.title: 6. TYPE HINTS
differences.leftDesc: Heavily relies on Python type hints for validation and clarity.
differences.rightDesc: Supports type hints but not as fundamental to its core design.

differences.title: 7. PROJECT TYPE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for building fast, modern web APIs and microservices.
differences.rightDesc: Best suited for complex web applications and content management systems.
